mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals stronger labor market conditions, boosting corporate profits and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals a supply-side imbalance that increases inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% m/m and HOUST falls > 2% m/m
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO rises >1.5% and HOUST falls >2%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) relative to GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening consumer demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS/GDPC1 ratio declines >1.2% m/m.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with low Fed funds rates predicts central bank inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL >3% and FEDFUNDS <1%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) in a low-unemployment environment signals overheating labor markets, prompting monetary tightening that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS >200k and UNRATE <3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) combined with stable CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 3% m/m and CPIAUCSL < 0.5% m/m
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST rises >3% and CPIAUCSL <0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) in the context of declining real sales (RSAFS) signals overcapacity and weak demand, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% m/m and RSAFS falls > 0.8% m/m
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO rises >1.5% and RSAFS falls >0.8%.
